qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Missing HTTP server timeouts enable Slowloris DoS via connection exhaustion
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: missing HTTP read-header timeouts in server startup, not tarball extraction path traversal in extractPackageTarball.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path Traversal - Arbitrary File Read via SubPath Parameter
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different vulnerable code and sink: router SubPath file serving reads arbitrary files, while the advisory is tarball extraction writing files outside the package directory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF via IPv6 Localhost Bypass in isLocalhost() Function
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Different root cause: incomplete localhost validation in isLocalhost for HTTP module loading, not unsafe tar entry path handling in extractPackageTarball.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Tarball extraction path traversal allows arbitrary file write via malicious npm package
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same root cause and sink as the advisory: extractPackageTarball derives a filesystem path from attacker-controlled tar entry names and writes outside the intended package directory.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: TarSlip Path Traversal Vulnerability in esm.sh Package Tarball Extraction
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Same vulnerable function and control-flow condition as the advisory: extractPackageTarball accepts malicious tar header paths and reaches ensureDir/os.OpenFile outside pkgDir.

selected_finding.title: Tarball extraction path traversal allows arbitrary file write via malicious npm package

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The selected finding identifies the advisory-described extractPackageTarball path traversal root cause and the copied PoC demonstrated attacker-controlled file write outside the intended package directory.
evidence: The advisory describes extractPackageTarball accepting malicious package tar paths and enabling writes outside the intended installation directory.
The selected report cites server/npmrc.go:548-549 and server/npmrc.go:562-563, where tar entry names are converted to paths and written with os.OpenFile without containment validation.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_4050692f/.judge/poc_runs/tarball_path_traversal/run.log records creation of a malicious tarball entry package/../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../../tmp/evohunt-pwned.js.

/Users/mac/ResearchSpace/opencode/EvoHunt-Run/advisory_4050692f/.judge/poc_runs/tarball_path_traversal/run.log records HTTP 200 for the malicious package request and confirms /tmp/evohunt-pwned.js contains EVOHUNT_POC_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESS.

poc_executions.observed_effect: The copied PoC built esm.sh, served a malicious npm tarball, requested evil-pkg@1.0.0, and confirmed /tmp/evohunt-pwned.js was written outside the package directory with the marker EVOHUNT_POC_PATH_TRAVERSAL_SUCCESS.
